definition: The New Keynesian Phillips Curve is convex near potential output: last mile disinflation from ~2.5-3% to the 2% target requires disproportionate output sacrifice (sacrifice ratio 1.5-3.0, potentially 4-5 for last mile) or a favorable supply shock. The 2022-2025 disinflation path is consistent — rapid decline from 5.6% to ~3% as supply chains normalized, then sticky.
evidence: Three exhaustive paths to 2%: negative output gap, favorable supply shock, or expectations re-anchoring
5Y5Y inflation expectations at ~2.3%, Michigan 5-10Y at ~2.5% — not fully anchored at 2%
Rapid disinflation from 5.6% to ~3% then stalling is consistent with convexity
pair_2 noted this was absent from Post-Keynesian framework — a gap
open_questions: What is the precise sacrifice ratio for the last mile of disinflation?
Can AI or immigration provide the favorable supply shock needed?
Will de-anchoring of inflation expectations create a stagflationary trap?
